page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-16 is a graphics-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 2 threads per core running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of a single NVIDIA L4 GPU from the Ada Lovelace architecture, which provides 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include any local storage and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a significant performance divergence between CPU-only tasks and GPU-accelerated operations. Single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average middle 50% tier. Conversely, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference speed, specifically during prompt processing and text generation with small and medium models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server is optimized for workloads that can actively leverage GPU acceleration to offset its weaker single-core CPU performance. The lack of local storage and lower CPU single-core metrics represent clear tradeoffs. It is best suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and parallelized data processing workloads rather than general-purpose CPU-bound applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-16 is a graphics-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. It does not include local storage. While single-core CPU benchmarks are weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, its GPU-accelerated LLM inference performance for small and medium models ranks in the top 10% tier. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for machine learning and graphics workloads, despite CPU-bound performance tradeoffs.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-16 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22GB VRAM, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-16 features 16 vCPUs, 64GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
tagline: A graphics-optimized virtual server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors with an NVIDIA L4 GPU for accelerated machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with 1 NVIDIA L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM
16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier performance in the top 10% for small and medium LLM inference
Weak single-core CPU performance represents a key hardware tradeoff
No local storage is bundled with this instance type
Suited for GPU-accelerated workloads, machine learning, and rendering
